latest.title: Further CEO Share Purchase
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 14:30
RNS Number : 0039T
Renalytix PLC
13 February 2026
Renalytix plc
("Renalytix" or the "Company")
Further CEO Share Purchase
LONDON and NEW YORK, 13 February 2026
- Renalytix plc (LSE: RENX) (OTCQB: RNLXY), a precision medicine diagnostics company, whose product
kidneyintelX.dkd
, is the only FDA-approved and Medicare-reimbursed prognostic test to support early-stage risk assessment in chronic diabetic kidney disease,
announces
that on 12 February 2026 James McCullough
, Chief Executive Officer, bought a further 750 American Depositary Shares (ADS) at an average price of US$3.15 per ADS.
ADS consist of 50 Ordinary Shares of £0.0025 each.
Following these purchases,
James McCullough
is interested in 3,430,096 Ordinary Shares1 representing 0.78% of the issued share capital of the Company.
1
James McCullough shareholding includes ordinary shares held through his family trust, The McCullough 2020 Irrevocable Trust.
For further information, please contact:
Renalytix plc
www.renalytix.com
James McCullough, CEO
Via Walbrook PR
SP Angel Corporate Finance LLP (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)
Tel:
+44 (0)20 3470 0470
Jeff Keating / David Hignell
(Corporate Finance)
Vadim Alexandre (Corporate Broking)
Oberon Capital (Joint Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 3179 5300
Mike Seabrook / Nick Lovering
Walbrook PR Limited
Tel: +44 (0)20 7933 8780 or
renalytix@walbrookpr.com
Paul McManus / Alice Woodings
Mob: +44 (0)7980 541 893 / +44 (0)7407 804 654
About Renalytix
(
www.renalytix.com
)
Renalytix (LSE: RENX) (OTCQB: RNLXY) is an artificial intelligence-enabled
in vitro
diagnostics company, focused on optimizing clinical management of kidney disease to drive improved patient outcomes. Renalytix has received FDA approval and Medicare reimbursement for
kidneyintelX.dkd
which is now offered commercially in the United States.
Unrecognized and uncontrolled diabetic kidney disease remains one of the largest barriers to controlling cost and suffering in the United States and the United Kingdom's medical system, affecting approximately 15 million and 8 million people, respectively. After five years of development and clinical validation,
kidneyintelX.dkd
is the only FDA-approved and Medicare-reimbursed prognostic tool capable of understanding a patient's risk with diabetic kidney disease early where treatment has maximal effect.
kidneyintelX.dkd
is now being deployed across large physician group practices and health systems in select regions of the United States.
The over 15,000 patients that have been tested by
kidneyintelX.dkd
have produced a substantial body of real-world performance data. In patient populations where
kidneyintelX.dkd
has been deployed, a demonstrated and significant increase in diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment rates have been recorded.
kidneyintelX.dkd
now has full reimbursement established by Medicare, the largest insurance payer in the United States, at $950 per reportable result.
kidneyintelX.dkd
is also recommended for use in the international chronic kidney disease clinical guidelines (KDIGO).
KidneyIntelX
is based on technology developed by Mount Sinai faculty and licensed to Renalytix AI, Inc. Mount Sinai faculty members are co-founders and equity owners in the Company. In addition, the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai has equity ownership in Renalytix.
For information about the kidneyintelX,dkd test, visit
kidneyintelx.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Renx Ltd is a medical‑diagnostics firm that develops blood‑based tests to identify and monitor kidney disease. Its main product line is a panel of biomarkers that can flag early kidney damage, helping clinicians decide when to start treatment or adjust medication.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Kidney disease is a growing global problem—over 10% of adults worldwide have some form of chronic kidney disease. Early detection saves patients costly dialysis and improves outcomes, so a reliable, quick test has a large market. Renx’s tests are designed to fit into routine blood work, giving it a competitive edge over more invasive or expensive alternatives.
company_30s.current_state: Renx is still in a survival phase. It has a modest cash runway that extends to June, but it has yet to generate revenue. The company is actively building clinical partnerships and seeking additional capital, while its share price reflects the uncertainty.

rns_narrative: The company is busy reshaping its shareholder base: a new investment from First Equity and a director‑level share purchase signal that insiders are still backing the business.

Operational updates show progress on clinical integrations—Renx is working with several hospitals to embed its tests into routine care, which could open a revenue stream if the partnerships materialise.
Cash‑runway disclosures confirm that the firm can survive until the end of the first half of the year, but that any new funding or a breakthrough partnership is needed to avoid a liquidity crisis.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but cautious optimism – traders are waiting for a clear sign that the company can turn the tide.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few voices point to the recent cash runway and the new clinical integration as reasons to hold on and wait for a positive announcement.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many commentators warn that the company still has no revenue and is burning cash. They fear that without a new funding round or a major partnership, the firm could face delisting or liquidation.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been largely static; no dramatic shift in tone has emerged yet, but the silence itself is a signal that traders are holding their breath.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Renx is flat and inactive, which means a news event could trigger a big surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new hospital partnership or a signed multi‑site study could validate the product’s market potential.
Any funding announcement—whether a private placement or a new shareholder investment—would extend the runway and boost confidence.
A regulatory update, such as a positive opinion from a national health authority on the test, would give the stock a clear catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden volume spike and a break above the recent low. A new RNS that confirms a partnership or funding will also be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price sits in the tail end of a deep crash. If volume has dried out and the share has found a support zone, the next bounce could come as soon as a catalyst surfaces, but it could also take months if the company remains cash‑constrained.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Renx sits at the bottom of a 70‑95% fall, which historically produces the biggest rebounds in our system. If the company secures a new partnership or funding, the price could recover to about half its former peak, giving a roughly 70% upside. The signal colour hierarchy suggests a solid crash bottom, and the lack of public sentiment means the market is primed for a surprise rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm has no revenue yet and a limited cash runway. If it fails to raise money or win a key partnership, it could hit a delisting threshold or file for insolvency. In that scenario the stock would stay near zero, giving a realistic worst‑case loss equal to the current price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 70% versus a downside that is capped at the current price. The asymmetry is clear: you can lose only what you invested, but you could gain a large premium if the catalyst triggers a bounce.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a volume surge that confirms a break above the recent low, size the position for 10–15% of your portfolio, and set a stop just below the support level. Target the midpoint between the current price and the previous high, which should give a comfortable risk‑reward ratio.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a partnership announcement or a funding round. Watch for a sharp uptick in trading volume and a price move above the last low; that would be your entry point.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, exit immediately. No trade should be taken if the company’s cash runway expires without any new capital infusion.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Renx is a deep‑crashed, cash‑constrained diagnostics start‑up that could rebound dramatically if it lands a new partnership or funding—offering a high‑reward, low‑downside play for contrarian swing traders.

industry_intelligence.name: Biotech & Pharma
recovery_paths: Orphan drug designation or fast track

panic_analysis.interpretation: 🟢 Normal volatility - not a crash signal
panic_analysis.opportunity_reason: 😐 Normal market conditions - no crash signal

fear_vs_facts.verdict: ⚖️ Mixed signals - Fear and facts balanced, wait for clarity

catalyst_pipeline.title: Watch for: Funding announcement or operational update

decision_matrix.reason: ⏳ Monitor for timing regime change or panic escalation

technical_score.reason: AI Technical Score 9.0/20 translates to 11/25 points
